<div class="section" id="dagster-cli">

# Dagster CLI

<div class="section" id="dagster-asset">

## dagster asset

Commands for working with Dagster assets.

    ```shell
    dagster asset [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>list</dt>
    <dd>
    List assets
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>materialize</dt>
    <dd>
    Execute a run to materialize a selection of assets
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>wipe</dt>
    <dd>

    Eliminate asset key indexes from event logs.

    > 

    Warning: Cannot be undone.

    Usage:

    > 

    dagster asset wipe –all

    dagster asset wipe \<unstructured_asset_key_name>

    dagster asset wipe \<json_string_of_structured_asset_key>

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>wipe-partitions-status-cache</dt>
    <dd>

    Clears the asset partitions status cache, which is used by the webserver to load partition

    > 

    pages more quickly. The cache will be rebuilt the next time the partition pages are loaded,

    if caching is enabled.

    Usage:

    > 

    dagster asset wipe-cache –all

    dagster asset wipe-cache \<unstructured_asset_key_name>

    dagster asset wipe-cache \<json_string_of_structured_asset_key>

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-debug">

## dagster debug

Commands for helping debug Dagster issues by dumping or loading artifacts from specific runs.

This can be used to send a file to someone like the Dagster team who doesn’t have direct access
to your instance to allow them to view the events and details of a specific run.

Debug files can be viewed using <cite>dagster-webserver-debug</cite> cli.
Debug files can also be downloaded from the Dagster UI.

    ```shell
    dagster debug [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>export</dt>
    <dd>
    Export the relevant artifacts for a job run from the current instance in to a file.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>import</dt>
    <dd>
    Import the relevant artifacts from debug files in to the current instance.
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-definitions-validate">
## dagster definitions validate

The <cite>dagster definitions validate</cite> command loads and validate your Dagster definitions using a Dagster instance.

This command indicates which code locations contain errors, and which ones can be successfully loaded.
Code locations containing errors are considered invalid, otherwise valid.

When running, this command sets the environment variable <cite>DAGSTER_IS_DEFS_VALIDATION_CLI=1</cite>.
This environment variable can be used to control the behavior of your code in validation mode.

This command returns an exit code 1 when errors are found, otherwise an exit code 0.

This command should be run in a Python environment where the <cite>dagster</cite> package is installed.

    ```shell
    dagster definitions validate [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-v'>-v, --verbose<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-v" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Show verbose stack traces, including system frames in stack traces.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-load-with-grpc'>--load-with-grpc<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-load-with-grpc" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Load the code locations using a gRPC server, instead of in-process.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-log-format'>--log-format \<log_format><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-log-format" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Format of the logs for dagster services

    Default: `'colored'`Options: colored | json | rich

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-log-level'>--log-level \<log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for dagster services.

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-empty-workspace'>--empty-workspace<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-empty-workspace" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Allow an empty workspace
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-w'>-w, --workspace \<workspace><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-w" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Path to workspace file. Argument can be provided multiple times.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file or files (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module or modules (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-port'>--grpc-port \<grpc_port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-port" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-socket'>--grpc-socket \<grpc_socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-socket" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Named socket to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-host'>--grpc-host \<grpc_host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-grpc-host" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use to connect to gRPC server, defaults to localhost
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-use-ssl'>--use-ssl<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-use-ssl" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use a secure channel when connecting to the gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-d)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-definitions-validate-a)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-dev">
## dagster dev

Start a local deployment of Dagster, including dagster-webserver running on localhost and the dagster-daemon running in the background

    ```shell
    dagster dev [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-code-server-log-level'>--code-server-log-level \<code_server_log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-code-server-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for code servers spun up by dagster services.

    Default: `'warning'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-log-level'>--log-level \<log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for dagster services.

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-log-format'>--log-format \<log_format><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-log-format" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Format of the logs for dagster services

    Default: `'colored'`Options: colored | json | rich

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-p'>-p, --port, --dagit-port \<port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-p" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use for the Dagster webserver.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-h'>-h, --host, --dagit-host \<host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-h" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use for the Dagster webserver.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-live-data-poll-rate'>--live-data-poll-rate \<live_data_poll_rate><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-live-data-poll-rate" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Rate at which the dagster UI polls for updated asset data (in milliseconds)

    Default: `'2000'`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-use-legacy-code-server-behavior'>--use-legacy-code-server-behavior<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-use-legacy-code-server-behavior" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use the legacy behavior of the daemon and webserver each starting up their own code server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-v'>-v, --verbose<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-v" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Show verbose stack traces for errors in the code server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-use-ssl'>--use-ssl<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-use-ssl" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use a secure channel when connecting to the gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-host'>--grpc-host \<grpc_host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-host" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use to connect to gRPC server, defaults to localhost
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-socket'>--grpc-socket \<grpc_socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-socket" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Named socket to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-port'>--grpc-port \<grpc_port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-grpc-port" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module or modules (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file or files (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-w'>-w, --workspace \<workspace><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-w" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Path to workspace file. Argument can be provided multiple times.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-dev-empty-workspace'>--empty-workspace<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-dev-empty-workspace" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Allow an empty workspace
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-a)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-dev-d)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-instance">

## dagster instance

Commands for working with the current Dagster instance.

    ```shell
    dagster instance [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>concurrency</dt>
    <dd>
    Commands for working with the instance-wide op concurrency.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>info</dt>
    <dd>
    List the information about the current instance.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>migrate</dt>
    <dd>
    Automatically migrate an out of date instance.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>reindex</dt>
    <dd>
    Rebuild index over historical runs for performance.
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-job">

## dagster job

Commands for working with Dagster jobs.

    ```shell
    dagster job [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>backfill</dt>
    <dd>

    Backfill a partitioned job.

    This commands targets a job. The job can be specified in a number of ways:

    1. dagster job backfill -j \<\<job_name>> (works if .workspace.yaml exists)
    2. dagster job backfill -j \<\<job_name>> -w path/to/workspace.yaml
    3. dagster job backfill -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_job
    4. dagster job backfill -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_job
    5. dagster job backfill -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>
    6. dagster job backfill -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>execute</dt>
    <dd>

    Execute a job.

    This commands targets a job. The job can be specified in a number of ways:

    1. dagster job execute -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_job
    2. dagster job execute -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_job
    3. dagster job execute -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>
    4. dagster job execute -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>launch</dt>
    <dd>

    Launch a job using the run launcher configured on the Dagster instance.

    This commands targets a job. The job can be specified in a number of ways:

    1. dagster job launch -j \<\<job_name>> (works if .workspace.yaml exists)
    2. dagster job launch -j \<\<job_name>> -w path/to/workspace.yaml
    3. dagster job launch -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_job
    4. dagster job launch -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_job
    5. dagster job launch -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>
    6. dagster job launch -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>list</dt>
    <dd>
    List the jobs in a repository. Can only use ONE of –workspace/-w, –python-file/-f, –module-name/-m, –grpc-port, –grpc-socket.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>print</dt>
    <dd>

    Print a job.

    This commands targets a job. The job can be specified in a number of ways:

    1. dagster job print -j \<\<job_name>> (works if .workspace.yaml exists)
    2. dagster job print -j \<\<job_name>> -w path/to/workspace.yaml
    3. dagster job print -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_job
    4. dagster job print -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_job
    5. dagster job print -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>
    6. dagster job print -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>scaffold_config</dt>
    <dd>

    Scaffold the config for a job.

    This commands targets a job. The job can be specified in a number of ways:

    1. dagster job scaffold_config -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_job
    2. dagster job scaffold_config -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_job
    3. dagster job scaffold_config -f /path/to/file.py -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>
    4. dagster job scaffold_config -m a_module.submodule -a define_some_repo -j \<\<job_name>>

    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-run">

## dagster run

Commands for working with Dagster job runs.

    ```shell
    dagster run [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>delete</dt>
    <dd>
    Delete a run by id and its associated event logs. Warning: Cannot be undone
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>list</dt>
    <dd>
    List the runs in the current Dagster instance.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>migrate-repository</dt>
    <dd>
    Migrate the run history for a job from a historic repository to its current repository.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>wipe</dt>
    <dd>
    Eliminate all run history and event logs. Warning: Cannot be undone.
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-schedule">

## dagster schedule

Commands for working with Dagster schedules.

    ```shell
    dagster schedule [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>debug</dt>
    <dd>
    Debug information about the scheduler.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>list</dt>
    <dd>
    List all schedules that correspond to a repository.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>logs</dt>
    <dd>
    Get logs for a schedule.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>preview</dt>
    <dd>
    Preview changes that will be performed by <cite>dagster schedule up</cite>.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>restart</dt>
    <dd>
    Restart a running schedule.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>start</dt>
    <dd>
    Start an existing schedule.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>stop</dt>
    <dd>
    Stop an existing schedule.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>wipe</dt>
    <dd>
    Delete the schedule history and turn off all schedules.
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-sensor">

## dagster sensor

Commands for working with Dagster sensors.

    ```shell
    dagster sensor [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>cursor</dt>
    <dd>
    Set the cursor value for an existing sensor.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>list</dt>
    <dd>
    List all sensors that correspond to a repository.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>preview</dt>
    <dd>
    Preview an existing sensor execution.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>start</dt>
    <dd>
    Start an existing sensor.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>stop</dt>
    <dd>
    Stop an existing sensor.
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-project">

## dagster project

Commands for bootstrapping new Dagster projects and code locations.

    ```shell
    dagster project [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```

Commands:

<dl>
    <dt>from-example</dt>
    <dd>
    Download one of the official Dagster examples to the current directory. This CLI enables you to quickly bootstrap your project with an officially maintained example.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>list-examples</dt>
    <dd>
    List the examples that available to bootstrap with.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>scaffold</dt>
    <dd>
    Create a folder structure with a single Dagster code location and other files such as pyproject.toml. This CLI enables you to quickly start building a new Dagster project with everything set up.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>scaffold-code-location</dt>
    <dd>
    (DEPRECATED; Use <cite>dagster project scaffold –excludes README.md</cite> instead) Create a folder structure with a single Dagster code location, in the current directory. This CLI helps you to scaffold a new Dagster code location within a folder structure that includes multiple Dagster code locations.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt>scaffold-repository</dt>
    <dd>
    (DEPRECATED; Use <cite>dagster project scaffold –excludes README.md</cite> instead) Create a folder structure with a single Dagster repository, in the current directory. This CLI helps you to scaffold a new Dagster repository within a folder structure that includes multiple Dagster repositories
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-graphql">
## dagster-graphql

Run a GraphQL query against the dagster interface to a specified repository or pipeline/job.

Can only use ONE of –workspace/-w, –python-file/-f, –module-name/-m, –grpc-port, –grpc-socket.

Examples:

1. dagster-graphql
2. dagster-graphql -w path/to/workspace.yaml
3. dagster-graphql -f path/to/file.py -a define_repo
4. dagster-graphql -m some_module -a define_repo
5. dagster-graphql -f path/to/file.py -a define_pipeline
6. dagster-graphql -m some_module -a define_pipeline

    ```shell
    dagster-graphql [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-version'>--version<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-version" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Show the version and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-t'>-t, --text \<text><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-t" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    GraphQL document to execute passed as a string
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-file'>--file \<file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-file" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    GraphQL document to execute passed as a file
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-p'>-p, --predefined \<predefined><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-p" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    GraphQL document to execute, from a predefined set provided by dagster-graphql.

    Options: launchPipelineExecution

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-v'>-v, --variables \<variables><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-v" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A JSON encoded string containing the variables for GraphQL execution.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-r'>-r, --remote \<remote><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-r" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A URL for a remote instance running dagster-webserver to send the GraphQL request to.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-o'>-o, --output \<output><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A file path to store the GraphQL response to. This flag is useful when making pipeline/job execution queries, since pipeline/job execution causes logs to print to stdout and stderr.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-ephemeral-instance'>--ephemeral-instance<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-ephemeral-instance" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use an ephemeral DagsterInstance instead of resolving via DAGSTER_HOME
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-use-ssl'>--use-ssl<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-use-ssl" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use a secure channel when connecting to the gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-host'>--grpc-host \<grpc_host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-host" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use to connect to gRPC server, defaults to localhost
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-socket'>--grpc-socket \<grpc_socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-socket" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Named socket to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-port'>--grpc-port \<grpc_port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-grpc-port" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module or modules (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file or files (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-w'>-w, --workspace \<workspace><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-w" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Path to workspace file. Argument can be provided multiple times.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-graphql-empty-workspace'>--empty-workspace<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-empty-workspace" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Allow an empty workspace
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-a)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-graphql-d)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-webserver">
## dagster-webserver

Run dagster-webserver. Loads a code location.

Can only use ONE of –workspace/-w, –python-file/-f, –module-name/-m, –grpc-port, –grpc-socket.

Examples:

1. dagster-webserver (works if ./workspace.yaml exists)
2. dagster-webserver -w path/to/workspace.yaml
3. dagster-webserver -f path/to/file.py
4. dagster-webserver -f path/to/file.py -d path/to/working_directory
5. dagster-webserver -m some_module
6. dagster-webserver -f path/to/file.py -a define_repo
7. dagster-webserver -m some_module -a define_repo
8. dagster-webserver -p 3333
Options can also provide arguments via environment variables prefixed with DAGSTER_WEBSERVER.

For example, DAGSTER_WEBSERVER_PORT=3333 dagster-webserver

    ```shell
    dagster-webserver [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-h'>-h, --host \<host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-h" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Host to run server on

    Default: `'127.0.0.1'`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-p'>-p, --port \<port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-p" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to run server on - defaults to 3000
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-l'>-l, --path-prefix \<path_prefix><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-l" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    The path prefix where server will be hosted (eg: /dagster-webserver)

    Default: `''`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-statement-timeout'>--db-statement-timeout \<db_statement_timeout><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-statement-timeout" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    The timeout in milliseconds to set on database statements sent to the DagsterInstance. Not respected in all configurations.

    Default: `15000`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-pool-recycle'>--db-pool-recycle \<db_pool_recycle><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-pool-recycle" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    The maximum age of a connection to use from the sqlalchemy pool without connection recycling. Set to -1 to disable. Not respected in all configurations.

    Default: `3600`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-pool-max-overflow'>--db-pool-max-overflow \<db_pool_max_overflow><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-db-pool-max-overflow" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    The maximum overflow size of the sqlalchemy pool. Set to -1 to disable.Not respected in all configurations.

    Default: `20`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-read-only'>--read-only<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-read-only" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Start server in read-only mode, where all mutations such as launching runs and turning schedules on/off are turned off.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-suppress-warnings'>--suppress-warnings<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-suppress-warnings" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Filter all warnings when hosting server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-uvicorn-log-level'>--uvicorn-log-level, --log-level \<uvicorn_log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-uvicorn-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for the uvicorn web server.

    Default: `'warning'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug | trace

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-dagster-log-level'>--dagster-log-level \<dagster_log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-dagster-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for dagster log events.

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-log-format'>--log-format \<log_format><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-log-format" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Format of the log output from the webserver

    Default: `'colored'`Options: colored | json | rich

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-code-server-log-level'>--code-server-log-level \<code_server_log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-code-server-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for any code servers spun up by the webserver.

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-live-data-poll-rate'>--live-data-poll-rate \<live_data_poll_rate><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-live-data-poll-rate" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Rate at which the dagster UI polls for updated asset data (in milliseconds)

    Default: `2000`

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-version'>--version<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-version" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Show the version and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-use-ssl'>--use-ssl<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-use-ssl" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use a secure channel when connecting to the gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-host'>--grpc-host \<grpc_host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-host" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use to connect to gRPC server, defaults to localhost
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-socket'>--grpc-socket \<grpc_socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-socket" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Named socket to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-port'>--grpc-port \<grpc_port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-grpc-port" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module or modules (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file or files (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-w'>-w, --workspace \<workspace><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-w" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Path to workspace file. Argument can be provided multiple times.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-webserver-empty-workspace'>--empty-workspace<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-empty-workspace" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Allow an empty workspace
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WEBSERVER_LOG_LEVEL</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--dagster-log-level`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-dagster-log-level)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-a)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-webserver-d)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-daemon-run">
## dagster-daemon run

Run any daemons configured on the DagsterInstance.

    ```shell
    dagster-daemon run [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-code-server-log-level'>--code-server-log-level \<code_server_log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-code-server-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for any code servers spun up by the daemon.

    Default: `'warning'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-log-level'>--log-level \<log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Set the log level for any code servers spun up by the daemon.

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-log-format'>--log-format \<log_format><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-log-format" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Format of the log output from the webserver

    Default: `'colored'`Options: colored | json | rich

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-use-ssl'>--use-ssl<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-use-ssl" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Use a secure channel when connecting to the gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-host'>--grpc-host \<grpc_host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-host" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Host to use to connect to gRPC server, defaults to localhost
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-socket'>--grpc-socket \<grpc_socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-socket" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Named socket to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-port'>--grpc-port \<grpc_port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-grpc-port" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port to use to connect to gRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module or modules (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file or files (flag can be used multiple times) where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load each file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-w'>-w, --workspace \<workspace><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-w" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Path to workspace file. Argument can be provided multiple times.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-empty-workspace'>--empty-workspace<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-empty-workspace" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Allow an empty workspace
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--log-level`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-log-level)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-a)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-daemon-run-d)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-daemon-wipe">

## dagster-daemon wipe

Wipe all heartbeats from storage.

    ```shell
    dagster-daemon wipe [OPTIONS]
    ```
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-daemon-debug-heartbeat-dump">

## dagster-daemon debug heartbeat-dump

Log all heartbeat statuses

    ```shell
    dagster-daemon debug heartbeat-dump [OPTIONS]
    ```
</div>

<div class="section" id="dagster-api-grpc">
## dagster api grpc

Serve the Dagster inter-process API over GRPC

    ```shell
    dagster api grpc [OPTIONS]
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-p'>-p, --port \<port><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-p" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Port over which to serve. You must pass one and only one of –port/-p or –socket/-s.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-s'>-s, --socket \<socket><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-s" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Serve over a UDS socket. You must pass one and only one of –port/-p or –socket/-s.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-h'>-h, --host \<host><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-h" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Hostname at which to serve. Default is localhost.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-n'>-n, --max-workers, --max_workers \<max_workers><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-n" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Maximum number of (threaded) workers to use in the GRPC server
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-heartbeat'>--heartbeat<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-heartbeat" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    If set, the GRPC server will shut itself down when it fails to receive a heartbeat after a timeout configurable with –heartbeat-timeout.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-heartbeat-timeout'>--heartbeat-timeout \<heartbeat_timeout><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-heartbeat-timeout" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Timeout after which to shutdown if –heartbeat is set and a heartbeat is not received
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-lazy-load-user-code'>--lazy-load-user-code<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-lazy-load-user-code" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Wait until the first LoadRepositories call to actually load the repositories, instead of waiting to load them when the server is launched. Useful for surfacing errors when the server is managed directly from the Dagster UI.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-use-python-environment-entry-point'>--use-python-environment-entry-point<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-use-python-environment-entry-point" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    If this flag is set, the server will signal to clients that they should launch dagster commands using <cite>\<this server’s python executable> -m dagster</cite>, instead of the default <cite>dagster</cite> entry point. This is useful when there are multiple Python environments running in the same machine, so a single <cite>dagster</cite> entry point is not enough to uniquely determine the environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-empty-working-directory'>--empty-working-directory<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-empty-working-directory" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Indicates that the working directory should be empty and should not set to the current directory as a default
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-fixed-server-id'>--fixed-server-id \<fixed_server_id><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-fixed-server-id" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    [INTERNAL] This option should generally not be used by users. Internal param used by dagster to spawn a gRPC server with the specified server id.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-log-level'>--log-level \<log_level><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-log-level" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Level at which to log output from the code server process

    Default: `'info'`Options: critical | error | warning | info | debug

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-log-format'>--log-format \<log_format><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-log-format" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Format of the log output from the code server process

    Default: `'colored'`Options: colored | json | rich

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-image'>--container-image \<container_image><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-image" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Container image to use to run code from this server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-context'>--container-context \<container_context><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-context" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Serialized JSON with configuration for any containers created to run the code from this server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-inject-env-vars-from-instance'>--inject-env-vars-from-instance<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-inject-env-vars-from-instance" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Whether to load env vars from the instance and inject them into the environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-location-name'>--location-name \<location_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-location-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Name of the code location this server corresponds to.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-instance-ref'>--instance-ref \<instance_ref><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-instance-ref" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    [INTERNAL] Serialized InstanceRef to use for accessing the instance
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-enable-metrics'>--enable-metrics<a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-enable-metrics" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    [INTERNAL] Retrieves current utilization metrics from GRPC server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-defs-state-info'>--defs-state-info \<defs_state_info><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-defs-state-info" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    [INTERNAL] Serialized DefsStateInfo to use for the server.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-a'>-a, --attribute \<attribute><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-a" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Attribute that is either a 1) repository or job or 2) a function that returns a repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-package-name'>--package-name \<package_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-package-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify Python package where repository or job function lives
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-autoload-defs-module-name'>--autoload-defs-module-name \<autoload_defs_module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-autoload-defs-module-name" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    A module to import and recursively search through for definitions.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-m'>-m, --module-name \<module_name><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-m" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify module where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load the module as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-f'>-f, --python-file \<python_file><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-f" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify python file where dagster definitions reside as top-level symbols/variables and load the file as a code location in the current python environment.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-d'>-d, --working-directory \<working_directory><a href="#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Specify working directory to use when loading the repository or job
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_GRPC_PORT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--port`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-p)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_GRPC_SOCKET</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--socket`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-s)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_GRPC_HOST</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--host`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-h)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_GRPC_MAX_WORKERS</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--max-workers`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-n)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_LAZY_LOAD_USER_CODE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--lazy-load-user-code`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-lazy-load-user-code)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_USE_PYTHON_ENVIRONMENT_ENTRY_POINT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--use-python-environment-entry-point`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-use-python-environment-entry-point)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_EMPTY_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--empty-working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-empty-working-directory)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CONTAINER_IMAGE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--container-image`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-image)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CONTAINER_CONTEXT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--container-context`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-container-context)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_INJECT_ENV_VARS_FROM_INSTANCE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--inject-env-vars-from-instance`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-inject-env-vars-from-instance)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_LOCATION_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--location-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-location-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_INSTANCE_REF</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--instance-ref`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-instance-ref)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ENABLE_SERVER_METRICS</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--enable-metrics`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-enable-metrics)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_ATTRIBUTE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--attribute`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-a)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PACKAGE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--package-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-package-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_autoload_defs_module_name</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--autoload-defs-module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-autoload-defs-module-name)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_MODULE_NAME</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--module-name`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-m)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_PYTHON_FILE</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--python-file`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-f)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_WORKING_DIRECTORY</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--working-directory`](#cmdoption-dagster-api-grpc-d)

</dd>

</dl>

<div class="section" id="create-dagster-cli">

# create-dagster CLI

<div class="section" id="installation">

## Installation

See the [Installation](https://docs.dagster.io/getting-started/installation) guide.

</div>

<div class="section" id="commands">

## Commands

<div class="section" id="create-dagster-project">

### create-dagster project

Scaffold a new Dagster project at PATH. The name of the project will be the final component of PATH.

This command can be run inside or outside of a workspace directory. If run inside a workspace,
the project will be added to the workspace’s list of project specs.

“.” may be passed as PATH to create the new project inside the existing working directory.

Created projects will have the following structure:

    ```default
    ├── src
    │   └── PROJECT_NAME
    │       ├── __init__.py
    │       ├── definitions.py
    │       ├── defs
    │       │   └── __init__.py
    │       └── components
    │           └── __init__.py
    ├── tests
    │   └── __init__.py
    └── pyproject.toml
    ```
The <cite>src.PROJECT_NAME.defs</cite> directory holds Python objects that can be targeted by the
<cite>dg scaffold</cite> command or have dg-inspectable metadata. Custom component types in the project
live in <cite>src.PROJECT_NAME.components</cite>. These types can be created with <cite>dg scaffold component</cite>.

Examples:

    ```default
    create-dagster project PROJECT_NAME
        Scaffold a new project in new directory PROJECT_NAME. Automatically creates directory
        and parent directories.
    create-dagster project .
        Scaffold a new project in the CWD. The project name is taken from the last component of the CWD.
    ```
    ```shell
    create-dagster project [OPTIONS] PATH
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-project-uv-sync'>--uv-sync, --no-uv-sync<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-project-uv-sync" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Preemptively answer the “Run uv sync?” prompt presented after project initialization.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-project-use-editable-dagster'>--use-editable-dagster<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-project-use-editable-dagster" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Install all Dagster package dependencies from a local Dagster clone. The location of the local Dagster clone will be read from the <cite>DAGSTER_GIT_REPO_DIR</cite> environment variable.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-project-verbose'>--verbose<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-project-verbose" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Enable verbose output for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-project-arg-PATH'>PATH<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-project-arg-PATH" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="create-dagster-workspace">

### create-dagster workspace

Initialize a new Dagster workspace.

The scaffolded workspace folder has the following structure:

    ```default
    ├── projects
    │   └── Dagster projects go here
    ├── deployments
    │   └── local
    │       ├── pyproject.toml
    │       └── uv.lock
    └── dg.toml
    ```
Examples:

    ```default
    create-dagster workspace WORKSPACE_NAME
        Scaffold a new workspace in new directory WORKSPACE_NAME. Automatically creates directory
        and parent directories.
    create-dagster workspace .
        Scaffold a new workspace in the CWD. The workspace name is the last component of the CWD.
    ```
    ```shell
    create-dagster workspace [OPTIONS] PATH
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-uv-sync'>--uv-sync, --no-uv-sync<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-uv-sync" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Preemptively answer the “Run uv sync?” prompt presented after project initialization.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-use-editable-dagster'>--use-editable-dagster<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-use-editable-dagster" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Install all Dagster package dependencies from a local Dagster clone. The location of the local Dagster clone will be read from the <cite>DAGSTER_GIT_REPO_DIR</cite> environment variable.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-verbose'>--verbose<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-verbose" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Enable verbose output for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-arg-PATH'>PATH<a href="#cmdoption-create-dagster-workspace-arg-PATH" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>
</div></div></div>

---

title: 'dg api reference'

description: 'dg api reference Dagster API | Comprehensive Python API documentation for Dagster, the data orchestration platform. Learn how to build, test, and maintain data pipelines with our detailed guides and examples.'

<div class="section" id="dg-api-reference">

# dg api reference

<div class="section" id="dg-api">

## dg api

Make REST-like API calls to Dagster Plus.

    ```shell
    dg api [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```
<div class="section" id="dg-api-agent">

### agent

Manage agents in Dagster Plus.

    ```shell
    dg api agent [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```
<div class="section" id="dg-api-agent-get">
#### get

Get detailed information about a specific agent.

    ```shell
    dg api agent get [OPTIONS] AGENT_ID
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-json'>--json<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-json" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Output in JSON format for machine readability
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-response-schema'>--response-schema<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-response-schema" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print the JSON Schema of the response model and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-o'>-o, --organization \<organization><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Organization to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>

<dl>

    <dd>
    Print GraphQL queries and responses to stderr for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-arg-AGENT_ID'>AGENT_ID<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-arg-AGENT_ID" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--organization`](#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-o)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--api-token`](#cmdoption-dg-api-agent-get-api-token)

</dd>

</dl>

<div class="section" id="dg-api-asset">

### asset

Manage assets in Dagster Plus.

    ```shell
    dg api asset [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```
<div class="section" id="dg-api-asset-get">
#### get

Get specific asset details.

    ```shell
    dg api asset get [OPTIONS] ASSET_KEY
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-status'>--status<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-status" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Include health and runtime status information
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-json'>--json<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-json" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Output in JSON format for machine readability
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-response-schema'>--response-schema<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-response-schema" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print the JSON Schema of the response model and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-d'>-d, --deployment \<deployment><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Deployment to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-o'>-o, --organization \<organization><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Organization to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Dagster Cloud API token.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-view-graphql'>--view-graphql<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-view-graphql" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print GraphQL queries and responses to stderr for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-arg-ASSET_KEY'>ASSET_KEY<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-arg-ASSET_KEY" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_DEPLOYMENT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--deployment`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-d)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--organization`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-o)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--api-token`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-api-token)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dg-api-asset-get-evaluations">
#### get-evaluations

Get automation condition evaluation records for an asset.

Evaluations are only recorded when at least one subcondition has a different
value than the previous evaluation.

    ```shell
    dg api asset get-evaluations [OPTIONS] ASSET_KEY
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-limit'>--limit \<limit><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-limit" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Max evaluations to return (default: 50, max: 1000)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-cursor'>--cursor \<cursor><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-cursor" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Cursor for pagination (evaluation ID)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-include-nodes'>--include-nodes<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-include-nodes" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Include the condition evaluation node tree (verbose)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-json'>--json<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-json" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Output in JSON format for machine readability
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-response-schema'>--response-schema<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-response-schema" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print the JSON Schema of the response model and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-d'>-d, --deployment \<deployment><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Deployment to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-o'>-o, --organization \<organization><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Organization to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Dagster Cloud API token.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-view-graphql'>--view-graphql<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-view-graphql" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print GraphQL queries and responses to stderr for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-arg-ASSET_KEY'>ASSET_KEY<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-arg-ASSET_KEY" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_DEPLOYMENT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--deployment`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-d)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--organization`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-o)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--api-token`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-evaluations-api-token)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

<div class="section" id="dg-api-asset-get-events">
#### get-events

Get materialization and observation events for an asset.

    ```shell
    dg api asset get-events [OPTIONS] ASSET_KEY
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-event-type'>--event-type \<event_type><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-event-type" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    Filter by event type (default: both)

    Options: ASSET_MATERIALIZATION | ASSET_OBSERVATION

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-limit'>--limit \<limit><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-limit" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Max events to return (default: 50, max: 1000)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-before'>--before \<before><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-before" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Events before this ISO timestamp (e.g. 2024-01-15T00:00:00)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-partition'>--partition \<partitions><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-partition" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Filter by partition key (repeatable)
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-json'>--json<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-json" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Output in JSON format for machine readability
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-response-schema'>--response-schema<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-response-schema" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print the JSON Schema of the response model and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-d'>-d, --deployment \<deployment><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-d" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Deployment to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-o'>-o, --organization \<organization><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Organization to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Dagster Cloud API token.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-view-graphql'>--view-graphql<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-view-graphql" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print GraphQL queries and responses to stderr for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-arg-ASSET_KEY'>ASSET_KEY<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-arg-ASSET_KEY" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_DEPLOYMENT</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--deployment`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-d)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--organization`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-o)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--api-token`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-get-events-api-token)

</dd>

</dl>
</div>

    </dd>

</dl>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-asset-list-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-list-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dd>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--organization`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-list-o)

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    > 

    Provide a default for [`--api-token`](#cmdoption-dg-api-asset-list-api-token)

</dd>

</dl>

<div class="section" id="dg-api-deployment">

### deployment

Manage deployments in Dagster Plus.

    ```shell
    dg api deployment [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    ```
<div class="section" id="dg-api-deployment-get">
#### get

Show detailed information about a specific deployment.

    ```shell
    dg api deployment get [OPTIONS] NAME
    ```

Options:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-json'>--json<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-json" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Output in JSON format for machine readability
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-response-schema'>--response-schema<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-response-schema" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print the JSON Schema of the response model and exit.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-o'>-o, --organization \<organization><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-o" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Organization to target.
    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-api-token'>--api-token \<api_token><a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-api-token" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>

    </dd>

</dl>
<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-view-graphql'>--view-graphql<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-view-graphql" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Print GraphQL queries and responses to stderr for debugging.
    </dd>

</dl>

Arguments:

<dl>
    <dt><Link class="anchor" id='cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-arg-NAME'>NAME<a href="#cmdoption-dg-api-deployment-get-arg-NAME" class="hash-link"></a></Link></dt>
    <dd>
    Required argument
    </dd>

</dl>

Environment variables:

<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION</dt>
    <dd>
    >

</dd>

</dl>
<dl>

    <dt>DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN</dt>
    <dd>
    >

</dd>

</dl>
</div>
